'''Grotto''' was the proper name of a less-than-competent [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] stationed at an outpost on [[Krake's Planet]].

==Biography==
Grotto had a close working relationship with another trooper named [[Turk]]. Turk however, seemed to spend most of his time repeatedly telling his friend to "Shaddup". When [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] learned that [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] had infiltrated the base in order to steal vital information concerning the identity of an agent named [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], he ordered Grotto and Turk to seize him "dead or alive". Grotto's unit found Skywalker downloading information from a databank in the kitchen area of the base and opened fire on him. Luke and his friend [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] retaliated by attacking the troopers directly. Grotto opened fire on Skywalker, but Luke grabbed Turk and used him as a shield against Grotto's blaster fire. After discarding Turk's body, Luke opened fire and blasted Grotto in the chest, killing him.

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Grotto was created by [[Marvel Comics]] writer, [[David Michelinie]]. Turk and Grotto were lampoons of two other Michelinie characters. In the 1980s, the Marvel Comics series, [[Wikipedia:Daredevil (Marvel Comics)|''Daredevil'']], regularly featured two incompetent snitches named ''Turk'' and ''Grotto'' who frequently found themselves staring into the business-end of Daredevil's billy-club. Like his Stormtrooper counterpart, Turk was often telling Grotto to "Shaddup".
